name: Kitchener Rice Cream
recipeInstructions:
To make a light pudding, use long grain rice. For a creamier pudding, use short grain rice.
Place the uncooked rice and the cream or milk in the top of a double boiler. Cover and cook for about 45 minutes until the rice is tender.
Beat the egg yolks until they are light yellow. Add 4 tablespoons sugar and beat until well blended. Add to the hot cooked rice, stirring constantly.
Simmer for 2 minutes. Keep stirring. Remove from heat and add the vanilla.
Beat the egg whites until stiff, then add 4 tablespoons sugar; beat until well blended and fold lightly into the rice cream. Remove from heat. Pour into a nice serving dish and serve hot. Or cover and chill until ready to serve cold.
